message:
feat(freeze): add LLVM LTO optimization for native-static builds

Integrate LLVM 20 LTO passes to optimize frozen Zig modules:
- Auto-detect LLVM 20 tools at /opt/homebrew/opt/llvm@20/bin
- Compile frozen_module.zig to bitcode with zig build-obj
- Apply lto<O3> passes with llvm opt for cross-function optimization
- Compile optimized bitcode to object with llc
- Localize duplicate symbols with llvm-objcopy
- Build.zig picks up frozen_module_opt.o when available

Performance improvement:
- Standard Zig: ~3135ms (12% slower than WAMR AOT)
- With LTO: ~2990ms (6.7% slower than WAMR AOT)
- Closes half the gap between pure Zig and WAMR AOT

Also includes:
- zig_hotpath_codegen.zig: Pure int32 hot path generator with noinline
- frozen_module_stub.zig: Stub for LTO object linking
- zig_hotpaths_stub.zig: Stub when hot paths not generated
- Iterator protocol support in zig_codegen_full.zig
- Closure variable support for Zig codegen

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+//! Stub module for LTO-optimized frozen module
+//! When using an externally compiled frozen_module_opt.o, this stub
+//! provides the type declarations needed for native_main_static.zig
+//!
+//! The actual implementation comes from frozen_module_opt.o at link time.
+
+const zig_runtime = @import("zig_runtime");
+
+// Declare frozen_init_c as external - it's defined in the LTO object file
+pub extern fn frozen_init_c(ctx: *zig_runtime.JSContext) void;

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+/// Stub module for zig_hotpaths when EDGEBOX_ZIG_HOTPATH is not enabled
+/// This gets replaced by the generated zig_hotpaths.zig when building with hot paths
+pub fn stub() void {}
